summary: Stellar second set with Saint, Space jam and soaring Stella Blue
blurb: The show rides a punchy first set into an inspired second half highlighted by Saint Of Circumstance, a Airto‑driven Space jam, and emotive renditions of The Last Time and Stella Blue. Jerry’s lyrical guitar and Phil’s “tractor‑beam” bass anchor the performance, while Vince’s keyboards stay dialed in. The Miller soundboard capture is crystal‑clear, making this one of the best late‑’91 recordings.
review: On December 30, 1991 at the Oakland Coliseum the Dead delivered a set that feels like a bridge between their early ’70s improvisational heights and the polished groove of the early ’90s. The first set opens with familiar fare—Touch of Grey, Row Jimmy and a bright Ramble On Rose—setting a lively tone that carries into a tight Promised Land finish. The second set truly shines: China Cat Sunflower segues into I Know You Rider before launching into Saint Of Circumstance, a brief jam, then the iconic Drum‑Space segment where Airto’s percussion adds an otherworldly texture. From there the band rides a soaring Stella Blue into a heartfelt turn on Your Lovelight, while The Last Time lands with quiet power. Jerry’s vocal phrasing and focused guitar flourishes sit perfectly atop Phil’s “raging giant tractor‑beam” bass, and Vince’s keyboard work remains dialed in throughout. With the Miller SBD capture offering crisp, clean sound that showcases every nuance, this show stands out as a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead’s late‑period brilliance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings (soundboard and high‑quality audience DAT) are clearly positive, praising multiple songs (Stella Blue, Saint Of Circumstance, Space jam, The Last Time) and band performance; the third recording is mixed but still notes a solid set despite poor audience tape. Consistent commendation across recordings yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Miller soundboard capture is described as "SICK" with no audio issues, delivering crisp and clean fidelity that best showcases the stellar performance.

key_highlights: Dear Prudence‑style jam (captured in the SBD) and tight Row Jimmy showcase late‑'91 energy
Saint Of Circumstance > Jam > Airto‑driven Drum/Space segment delivers a standout improvisational peak
Stella Blue and The Last Time provide emotive, soaring finishes to the second set
Miller soundboard is excellent; audience DAT also offers surprisingly clear mids and bass

band_performance.Jerry: Lyrical, focused guitar work with strong vocal phrasing; his playing feeds off Phil’s bass energy and stays on point throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers a powerful "tractor‑beam" bass that anchors the band, driving both the first‑set groove and the expansive second‑set jams.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard work is dialed in and fine, providing tasteful texture that complements Hornsby’s contributions without overpowering the mix.
band_performance.Mickey: Adds color and drive to the Airto‑led Drum/Space jam, enhancing the percussive landscape of the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Provides solid rhythmic foundation and joins Mickey in the dynamic percussion segment, keeping the groove tight during improvisations.
